summary: PR 2527 split the tail of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c into ten new ftCo_800Cxxxx translation units, promoted ft_0C31.c and the new units to Matching in configure.py, updated splits/symbol metadata for the new text and data ownership, and retargeted includes/prototypes in fighter, ftCommon, character init, and item files. The only discussion evidence is the decomp-dev bot report showing +2192 matched code bytes, +8128 linked code bytes, and 55 new matches, with many reported broken matches in old ft_0C31 because symbols moved into the new split units.

key_files.role: Split ft_0C31 text/data ranges and added ranges for ftCo_800C703C through ftCo_800C7CA0; moved one sbss range to ft_0C88.
Adjusted local/global symbol names, .sbss sizes, and .sdata2 labels around ftCo/ftCh/it data affected by the split.
Changed ft_0C31.c from NonMatching to Matching and added ten new Matching ftCo split objects.
Retained earlier ft_0C31 code and removed functions from ftCo_800C703C through ftCo_800C7CA0 into new files.
Removed prototypes for the functions split into the new ftCo headers.
New split unit containing ftCo_800C703C.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C703C.
New split unit containing ftCo_800C7070 and related motion-state code.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C7070.

New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C70D0 family.
New split unit for ftCo_800C7178 family functions.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C7178 family.
New split unit for ftCo_800C7220, ftCo_800C7294, and ftCo_800C7308 matches.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C7220 family.
New split unit for ftCo_800C739C.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C739C.
New split unit for ftCo_800C7434 family, including fn_800C74CC, ftCo_800C74F4, and fn_800C7568 matches.
New address-scoped header for ftCo_800C7434 family.
New split unit for ftCo_800C7590, ftCo_800C7800, and CaptureLeadead callbacks.
New address-scoped header used by itleadead and motion-state code.
New split unit for ftCo_800C78B0 family and CaptureLikelike callbacks.
New address-scoped header with typed prototypes including Vec3 and lbColl_80008D30_arg1 for ftCo_800C7B0C.
New split unit for down-reflect/down-bound related functions and ft_804D6580 data declaration.
New address-scoped header exporting ftCo_800C7CA0, fn_800C7DC4, DownReflect callbacks, and ft_804D6580.
Added fields to ftCommon_MotionVars near the rebound/capturelikelike region.
Replaced broad ft_0C31 include with the specific ftCo_800C7434 header.
Replaced broad ft_0C31 include with ftCo_800C7CA0 header.
Replaced broad ft_0C31 include with ftCo_800C7CA0 header.
Retargeted include from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C7434.
Retargeted includes from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C7220 and ftCo_800C739C.
Retargeted includes from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C70D0 and ftCo_800C7178.

Added ftCo_800C7CA0 include.
Retargeted include from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C703C.
Retargeted include from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C7CA0.
Retargeted include from ft_0C31 to ftCo_800C7CA0 and added related includes.
Header surface changed for ft_0C8C-related dependencies.
Small include/reference updates associated with split dependencies; bot report notes one .sdata broken match in ftcpuattack.
Added includes for multiple new ftCo split headers while retaining ft_0C31 include.
Adjusted includes to ft_0C8C and ftCo_800C7CA0.
Added ftCo_800C7590, ftCo_800C78B0, and ftCo_800C7CA0 includes.
Retargeted include to ftCo_800C7590 and made small code updates near it_802EA9FC.
Header signature/dependency adjustment for leadead capture interactions.
Retargeted include to ftCo_800C78B0.

what_changed: Split ft_0C31.c at 0x800C703C into ten new ftCo_800Cxxxx units and shortened ft_0C31.c split range to 0x800C61B0-0x800C703C.
Added new split ranges for ftCo_800C703C, ftCo_800C7070, ftCo_800C70D0, ftCo_800C7178, ftCo_800C7220, ftCo_800C739C, ftCo_800C7434, ftCo_800C7590, ftCo_800C78B0, and ftCo_800C7CA0.
Changed configure.py so ft_0C31.c and all ten new ftCo units are Matching objects.
Moved many prototypes out of ft_0C31.h into new address-scoped ftCo headers, and retargeted callsite includes to the narrower headers.
Updated symbols.txt for split-related local labels, float/double .sdata2 symbols, string/data symbol locality, and .sbss object sizes.
Bot report recorded 55 new matches and overall matched-code improvement, while showing broken matches in old ft_0C31 that correspond to functions moved to new units.

smart_moves.text: The split was coordinated across splits.txt, configure.py, headers, and callsite includes, avoiding a source-only move without build/split metadata.
The new headers narrowed dependencies after removing the large ft_0C31.h export surface; many callsites now include only the ftCo split header they use.
The PR adjusted symbol metadata alongside the TU split rather than leaving old ftCo_804D... or oversized sbss symbols attached to the old layout.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a formerly monolithic fighter TU is split into address-sized matching units, update split ranges, build object classification, exported prototypes, callsite includes, and symbol metadata as one reviewable change.

Bot reports for a TU split can show old-unit broken matches that are offset by new-unit matches; interpret the old-unit breakage together with the new split-unit match list rather than as ordinary regressions.
Replacing a broad legacy header with specific split headers reduces dependency surface after moving functions out of a monolithic file.

naming_conventions.convention: New split files and headers use address-style names matching their first function address, e.g. ftCo_800C703C.c/.h through ftCo_800C7CA0.c/.h.
New header guards use GALE01_<address>, e.g. GALE01_800C703C and GALE01_800C7CA0.
Unproven helper names remained address-style or fn_ names, while some known callbacks use semantic suffixes such as CaptureLeadead_Anim, CaptureLikelike_Anim, and DownReflect_Anim/Coll/Phys.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: TU splitting by exact text/data ranges: ft_0C31.c was shortened and ten contiguous ftCo split units were added with their own .sdata2 and, for ftCo_800C7CA0, .sbss ownership.
Use matching object classification in configure.py only after the split units and remaining ft_0C31.c were arranged as matching objects.
Keep address comments in public prototypes, e.g. /* 7CA0 */ and /* 7DC4 */, to preserve correspondence between split headers and binary addresses.

Recover more specific prototypes during header split; ftCo_800C7B0C changed from an UNK_RET/void-pointer style declaration in ft_0C31.h to a typed prototype using Vec3 and lbColl_80008D30_arg1 in ftCo_800C78B0.h.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 68.47% (+0.06%, +2192 bytes), linked code 40.46% (+0.21%, +8128 bytes), matched data 39.77% (+0.01%, +152 bytes), linked data 33.51% (+0.02%, +184 bytes), and 55 new matches.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The same report lists 36 broken matches and 6 unmatched regressions, mostly under old unit main/melee/ft/ft_0C31, consistent with moved symbols but not independently explained by a human comment in the PR evidence.
No review comments or human issue comments were present in the loaded PR slice; reviewer intent and requested changes are not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: PR 2527 ft_0C31 split broken matches old unit new matches
GALE01_800C703C header guard address-style split headers

notes: The PR body is empty and there are no loaded human review comments, so intent beyond the changed files and bot report is not evidenced.
The loaded raw diff is truncated in the prompt, but targeted local diff lookups supplied exact snippets for configure.py, headers, split files, and include retargeting.
No build logs, objdiff output, or manual verification comments were loaded beyond the decomp-dev bot summary; match claims are limited to that report.
The bot report lists broken matches/regressions; this intake treats them as report facts and does not independently validate whether each is harmless split churn.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Coordinate TU splits across splits, build config, headers, includes, and symbols
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2527 is evidence that a fighter TU split required synchronized updates to config/GALE01/splits.txt, configure.py Matching objects, new split headers, callsite includes, ft_0C31.h prototype removal, and symbols.txt metadata.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret old-unit broken matches carefully after a split
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR 2527 bot report showed many broken matches in old ft_0C31 while also reporting 55 new matches in the new ftCo split units and net matched-code improvement.

source_update_candidates.title: ft_0C31 split boundary at 0x800C703C from PR 2527
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2527 changed melee/ft/ft_0C31.c from text 0x800C61B0-0x800C8170 to 0x800C61B0-0x800C703C and created ftCo_800C703C.c through ftCo_800C7CA0.c for the following contiguous ranges.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-owned path/split fact and should be curated as path metadata, not inferred as a global decomp rule.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo split headers use address-scoped GALE01 guards
source_update_candidates.text: New headers such as ftCo_800C703C.h and ftCo_800C7CA0.h use header guards of the form GALE01_<first-function-address> and retain address comments on prototypes.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as local naming/path convention evidence for this split family; curator should decide whether it generalizes.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split match reports can show old-unit churn
source_update_candidates.text: For TU-splitting matching PRs, review bot broken-match entries in the old unit alongside new split-unit matches before classifying them as regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Supported by this PR's bot report, but one PR is only candidate evidence for a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not claim all bot-listed broken matches were harmless; the evidence only shows a net improvement and moved-function pattern.
Do not promote placeholder includes or local inline helper names as preferred style; the PR evidence shows their presence but not a reviewed convention.
Do not treat broad symbol locality changes in symbols.txt as a general rule without curator review of section ownership evidence.
